_meta.purpose: Plan presets ONLY. These are starter values a doctor copies into a free-form treatment plan; they are NOT first-class entities, do NOT have a lifecycle, and do NOT version. Per-child plans are authored fresh from a preset and individualized.

presets.case_label: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) with Sensory Processing Difficulties
presets.presenting_concerns: Hypersensitivity to light touch and loud sounds; toe-walking; poor eye contact; meltdowns in new or busy environments; avoids messy/wet textures; limited functional play skills; rigid routines.
presets.assessment_findings_template: SP2: Significant sensory sensitivity and sensory avoiding patterns. BOT-2: Bilateral coordination at 2nd percentile. CARS-2: Moderate autism. Notable proprioceptive and vestibular seeking behaviors observed.
presets.approach_notes: Ayres Sensory Integration (ASI); DIR/Floortime; Snoezelen multi-sensory room sessions (20 min x 3/week); Sensory Diet Protocol; parent coaching in naturalistic sensory strategies.

session_structure.label: Arrival & regulation check
Heavy work (proprioceptive input — wall push-ups, bear walks, animal walks)
Sensory circuit (swinging, tactile, balance)
Fine motor / structured play
Cool-down & transition preparation

presets.home_program: Wilbarger brushing protocol 2x daily; 10 min movement break before school; weighted blanket at bedtime; chewy tool during homework; parent coaching session monthly; written sensory diet schedule provided.

outcome_measures.schedule: Parent-reported meltdown frequency log (weekly); therapist behavioural observation checklist

short_term_goals_template: Tolerate light touch to hands 3/5 trials without distress
Engage in sand/rice tactile bin play for 5 minutes
Imitate 3-step motor sequence with 1 verbal prompt
Reduce meltdown frequency by 30% (parent log)
long_term_goals_template: Independent participation in group sensory activities at school
Independently use 2+ self-regulation strategies when dysregulated
Age-appropriate fine motor skills — pre-writing readiness
Tolerance of varied food textures at mealtimes

presets.case_label: Cerebral Palsy (Spastic Diplegia), GMFCS Level II
presets.presenting_concerns: Increased muscle tone in bilateral lower limbs; scissor gait pattern; poor hand function for classroom tasks; difficulty with self-care (buttons, zippers, shoe-tying); tires easily during academic tasks; low endurance.
presets.assessment_findings_template: PDMS-2: Fine motor age equivalent 3.5 years. WeeFIM Self-Care: 28/56 (moderate assistance required). MAS spasticity score: Lower limbs 2, Upper limbs 1. Functional grip strength reduced bilaterally.
presets.approach_notes: Constraint-Induced Movement Therapy (CIMT); task-oriented training; Neurodevelopmental Technique (NDT); dynamic splinting program; assistive technology (laptop, adapted pencils); graded activity for endurance.

session_structure.label: Warm-up & passive ROM exercises
Functional task practice — bilateral UE activities, fine motor tasks
ADL simulation (dressing, self-care practice)
Cool-down & home program review

presets.home_program: Passive stretching routine 2x daily (10 min — provided handout); practice dressing tasks at same time each day (habit stacking); splint wearing schedule per OT protocol; weekly video check-in available; school occupational therapy teacher liaison letter provided.

outcome_measures.schedule: School occupational performance checklist; handwriting speed & legibility measure

short_term_goals_template: Button shirt independently 4/5 attempts in 3 minutes
Maintain functional pencil grip with adapted tool for 10 min writing task
Don and doff AFO/orthotics with minimal assistance
Legible handwriting output for short classroom tasks
long_term_goals_template: Full independence in school-day ADLs
Legible handwriting at grade-level speed
Sustained fine motor endurance for full school day
Community ambulation with assistive device as needed

presets.case_label: ADHD with Sensory Seeking & Poor Attention Regulation
presets.presenting_concerns: Cannot sit still in class; constantly seeking movement — jumping, crashing, spinning; poor and illegible handwriting; frequently loses belongings; emotional outbursts during transitions; difficulty following multi-step instructions; peer conflict.
presets.assessment_findings_template: SP2: Sensation seeking quadrant significantly elevated (>2 SD). Beery VMI: 5th percentile. COPM: School performance 2/10 (performance), 3/10 (satisfaction). Teacher Connors: Elevated hyperactivity/impulsivity subscale.
presets.approach_notes: Sensory diet — Alert Program (How Does Your Engine Run?); Zones of Regulation (self-regulation curriculum); Handwriting Without Tears (HWT); executive function skill-building; classroom consultation and teacher education.

session_structure.label: Heavy work (wall push-ups, bear walks, resistance band — proprioceptive loading)
Fine motor & handwriting (HWT approach)
Self-regulation skills — Zones of Regulation activities
Executive function games & organizational skill practice

presets.home_program: Morning proprioceptive routine (10 min before school); dedicated quiet homework station with visual schedule; visual timer for task chunks (10 min work, 3 min movement break); color-coded folder system at home and school; teacher briefing note and strategy sheet provided; monthly review with parents.

outcome_measures.schedule: Teacher Connors rating scale (monthly); handwriting legibility and speed checklist; self-regulation incident log

short_term_goals_template: Remain seated at desk for 15 min using 1 scheduled movement break
Complete classroom writing task within allotted time with legible output
Independently use 2 self-regulation strategies when recognizing dysregulation
Follow 3-step verbal instruction 4/5 trials
long_term_goals_template: Legible & functional handwriting for full academic output
Self-regulate through 80% of school-day transitions independently
Organizational system maintained for school materials (daily check)
Positive peer interactions without adult prompt in structured activities

presets.case_label: Down Syndrome with Global Developmental Delay
presets.presenting_concerns: Low muscle tone (hypotonia) throughout body; significantly delayed fine and gross motor milestones; feeding difficulties — tongue thrust and poor oral-motor control; limited hand manipulation skills; short attention span for structured activities; delayed self-care skills.
presets.assessment_findings_template: PDMS-2: Gross motor age equivalent 12 months; Fine motor age equivalent 10 months. PEDI-CAT: Self-care domain severely limited. Oral-motor assessment: tongue thrust present, poor lip closure, limited jaw grading.
presets.approach_notes: Play-based early intervention (family-centred); oral-motor therapy (pre-feeding skills); Neurodevelopmental Technique (NDT) to address hypotonia; sensory-motor activities to build muscle tone and postural control; family coaching and parent education at every session.

session_structure.label: Tummy time & gross motor warm-up (strengthening, postural control)
Fine motor play activities (grasping, pinching, stacking, manipulation)
Oral-motor exercises & feeding skill practice
Parent coaching — reviewing home strategies

presets.home_program: Tummy time 3x daily (10 min — graded positioning); practice spoon feeding at every mealtime; oral-motor exercises before meals (therapist-guided programme); daily cause-effect toy / book play (10 min); parent coaching at every session; monthly multidisciplinary team review.

outcome_measures.schedule: Developmental milestone tracker (parent daily log); oral-motor feeding skills checklist

short_term_goals_template: Consistent pincer grasp for small objects 3/5 trials
Spoon-feed soft/mashed food with minimal assistance (wrist guidance)
Stack 3 blocks independently
Engage with cause-effect toy for 5 min (sustained attention)
long_term_goals_template: Self-feed full meal with spoon and fork with supervision only
Age-appropriate play skills in parallel play with same-age peers
Independent dressing skills for simple garments (pull-on pants, socks)
Communicative gestures and joint attention during play

presets.presenting_concerns: Clumsy — frequent trips and collisions; illegible and slow handwriting significantly below class peers; very slow to complete written tasks in school; difficulty learning new motor skills (bicycle, catching, swimming); actively avoids PE and sports; low self-esteem around physical activities; social withdrawal.
presets.assessment_findings_template: MABC-2: 3rd percentile (significant motor impairment). Beery VMI: 7th percentile. BOT-2: Running speed/agility 4th percentile. Parent and teacher report: avoids physical play, negative self-talk about motor skills.
presets.approach_notes: CO-OP (Cognitive Orientation to daily Occupational Performance) — child-led goal setting and strategy discovery; task-specific practice with errorless learning; motor imagery training; Handwriting Without Tears (HWT); self-monitoring strategies; classroom consultation & school letter.

session_structure.label: Warm-up & body awareness activities (proprioceptive, motor planning games)
Handwriting & fine motor skills (HWT approach, meaningful writing tasks)
Gross motor skill practice using CO-OP — Goal-Plan-Do-Check framework
Self-reflection and personal goal-setting with child

presets.home_program: Daily 10-min handwriting practice (structured — not freeform); teacher education letter provided with strategies for classroom support; classroom seating assessment (preferred front positioning); encourage swimming, cycling, or martial arts (lower collision-risk sports); practice chosen CO-OP skill daily (5 min); monthly parent review meeting.

outcome_measures.schedule: Handwriting speed & legibility checklist (monthly); PEGS — child self-report; PE teacher observation report

short_term_goals_template: Produce legible handwriting at functional speed for classroom tasks
Catch a tennis ball 7/10 trials at 2 metres distance
Navigate school stairs safely without holding railing
Manage full lunchbox and utensils independently
long_term_goals_template: Active participation in PE class activities alongside peers
Consistent written output at grade-level speed and legibility
Positive self-efficacy statements around physical and motor activities
Independent organization of school materials and schedule
